calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-30-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared via zoom for Jaime Bishop (administrator).

Mr. Wrobel stated that he filed a declaration yesterday to address the examiner's notes, that his client made several trips from Petaluma to San Francisco, and that the property is in poor condition. The first and final account and report of administrator etc., is taken under submission with the consideration of counsel's representation.

Judge: Stephen Murphy: Clerk: A. Datt: Deputy: Prieto: Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-03-2023 Continued To Nov-07-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared via zoom for Jaime Bishop(petitioner).
Matthew Tuller (GAL) appeared via zoom for Charie Klein (minor beneficiary).

Mr. Wrobel stated that he has a declaration prepared that covers all the examiners issues, that Donald doesn't need a special needs trust, but Jaime Bishop has special needs trust, and that Mr. Wrobel will file a final distribution as well. First and final account and report of administrator etc., is continued to November 07, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Papers to be filed by October 24, 2023.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Cabrera. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On The Close Of Estate And Creation Of A Pooled Special Needs Trust For Donald Bishop
Off Calendar Jun-28-2023 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On The Close Of Estate And Creation Of A Pooled Special Needs Trust For Donald Bishop
Off Calendar Apr-19-2023 Continued To Jun-28-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared by zoom for Jamie Bishop (administrator).
Matthew Tuller (Guardian Ad Litem for Charie Klein ("GAL")) appeared by zoom in propria persona.

The court asked for an update on Mr. Wrobel's Client's efforts to close this estate. Mr. Wrobel stated that at this point they are filing two affidavits for the personal property of James Bishop and Sharise Bishop, that the minor turns 18 on December 20, 2023, and that there is a question of needing to file for a guardianship of her estate, or proceeding with a GAL. Mr. Wrobel also stated that he is waiting for a signature from the GAL, and that once the affidavits are accepted he is prepared to go forward with final distribution. In addition, Mr. Wrobel spoke of making distribution to a custodial account that is set up by a custodial parent, stated that he can get the affidavits filed within a few weeks, that he can file for final distribution within 45-days, and that they are in communication with a Special Needs Trust Provider regarding Donald. Mr. Tuller stated that he agrees with putting the minor's distribution in a custodial account. Status on close of estate is continued to June 28, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The affidavits and a final petition are to be filed by June 14, 2023. Status of appointment of a personal representative of James Bishop and Sharice Bishop is off calendar.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Aeyoung Kim. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1467 Mckinnon Ave., San Francisco, Ca 94124)
Off Calendar Nov-02-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel, via Zoom, for Jamie Bishop, Personal Representative.

The court points to the issue of the defective bond and of the buyers only depositing 3% of the purchase price and not 10% as set forth in the terms of the published notice of sale. Counsel acknowledges the defective bond issue and advises that he has confirmation from the bonding company that the amended bond issue will be resolved forthwith and that the additional bond will be ready to file if the sale is approved by the court. The agent for the bidders, Stephanie Ruan, and the seller, Chavone Guthery, advise that they believed that the 10% deposit only applied to any overbidders and that an initial deposit in the amount of 10% had been submitted but was pulled and resubmitted at 3%. The court acknowledges the filing of waiver of notice by one of the bidders, Yudi Zhong. The court confirms with buyer Zhi Jian Lan on the record that he signed a copy of the waiver of notice which was rejected by the clerk's office because it was not dated.

The sale was confirmed at $700,000 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $647,000 is required.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Sep-08-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared by zoom for Jamie Bishop.

The court questions whether Mr. Leestma and his client are no longer objecting. Mr. Worth stated that he has not heard from anyone who is objecting, that they have been pushing them to put the property up for sale, that they don't have an accepted offer yet, and that he thinks Mr. Leestma's concerns have been alleviated. The court questions how distribution can be made to Charie without a personal representative for James and for Sharice. Mr. Worth stated that there is no personal representative for either one of them, that he can work on it, that he has attempted to get a guardian-ad-litem appointed, that he filed an ex parte petition in January, but he never heard anything and that he can re-file it. Mr. Worth also stated that they have a mentally disabled senior who has lived in the house all of his life, that the administrator has been working on finding housing for him, that some of the heirs have taken an advance, that there is a sewer issue, that it needs to be repaired and that some of the heirs are unable to contribute. In addition, Mr. Worth stated that there is an abatement issue which involves the City, and that some of the heirs would have preferred to keep the home, but it is an unfortunate situation. Discussion was held regarding using the equity in the property to buy the others out. The court informed Mr. Worth that it will be setting a status hearing on the appointment of personal representatives for James and Sharice. Further discussion was held regarding the need to establish a special needs trust for Donald and the need for Mr. Worth to follow up the issue of the appointment of a guardian-ad-litem. The Second Report on Status of Estate Administration and Petition for Extension of Time to Administer Estate is Under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-11-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Leon Ainer for Shamauda Bishop (Petitioner).
Thomas Wrobel for and with Jaimie Bishop.

Mr. Ainer stated that over the past few days he received notes from the Examiner requesting that they supplement the petition because Nathaniel was not in the petition, questions why the same was not asked of Mr. Wrobel and stated that Mr. Wrobel's declaration of real property also excluded some of the Beneficiaries, but again nothing was asked of him. Mr. Wrobel stated that he filed an amended declaration of real property naming Nathaniel, that James Bishop passed away after the Decedent and died intestate, that his petition has been in order and that Jaimie is ready to proceed in administering the estate. Mr. Wrobel also stated that most of the Heirs have nominated Jaimie and that Donald the Son of the Decedent also nominated Jaimie. Mr. Ainer stated that Donald is incapacitated and lacks capacity, that Donald cannot talk and questions how Donald could have signed and whether he understood the nomination. Mr. Wrobel stated that Donald can talk, that Donald has diminished capacity, that Donald is not a conservatee and that there is nothing that legally establishes that he lacks capacity. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration appointing Jaimie Bishop as the Administrator of the estate with limited authority (subject to review of the order). Bond in the amount of $20,000.00 is required. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is set for April 15, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on close of estate is set for December 16, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-25-2019 Continued To Dec-09-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Leon Ainer appeared by telephone for Shamauda Bishop (Petitioner).
Thomas Wrobel for and with Jaimie Bishop (Petitioner).

Mr. Wrobel informed the Court that several other Heirs are also present. The Court noted that it does not have courtesy copies of the petition, the notice or a proposed order from Mr. Ainer. Mr. Ainer stated that the only outstanding issue is bond, that he has served everyone, that he sent in his courtesy copies over a week ago and that he is having an issue with receiving ackowlegement of receipt of the courtesy copies. Mr. Wrobel stated that his petition is filed and is thorough, that a majority of the Heirs have nominated his Client and that he is ready to go on his petition. Both Petitions for Letters of Administration are continued to December 9, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Counsel that with or without courtesy copies it will make a ruling at the next hearing and advised Mr. Ainer to come in person to the Court at least one week prior to the next hearing to confirm that courtesy copies have been received.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
